name: Rachel Kim
notes: Pitchfork BNM designation changes careers. Pitch 3-4 weeks before release with private link. Story angle required — not just sonic description.

name: Marcus Webb
notes: Covers mainstream breakthrough acts. Prefers artists with existing press trail and touring history. Strong EPK required.

name: Zoe Hartman
beat: New Music, Emerging Artists, UK Scene
notes: UK-focused but covers global indie scene. 4-week lead time for features. Prefers artists with UK touring plans.

name: David Chen
notes: Data-driven editorial. Include streaming numbers, social growth metrics. Strong angle needed beyond the music.

name: Aisha Nwosu
notes: High-volume editorial. Song premieres preferred for debut singles. 2-week lead time. Include high-res artwork.

name: Tom Aldridge
notes: Style and culture angle as important as the music. Strong visual identity required. Prefers photo-forward pitches.

name: Sofia Delacroix
notes: Pitchfork-adjacent indie audience. Prefers exclusive premieres. 2-week notice for premiere scheduling.

name: Kemi Adebayo
notes: Black music-focused editorial. Culture and community angle resonates. Cover story candidates need existing fanbase.

name: Lena Fischer
notes: UK indie institution. Detailed artist bio and interview availability required. Covers undiscovered artists more than major labels.

name: Rico Martinez
notes: Youth culture publication. Authenticity essential. Social media presence matters. Include YouTube/TikTok metrics.

name: Hannah Cole
notes: UK indie DIY community. Strong live reputation helps. Accepts self-released artists. 3-week lead time.

name: Jordan Park
notes: Deep dive format — artist breaks down one song in detail. 6-8 week booking window. Artist must be willing to be vulnerable about process.

name: Imani Brooks
notes: 85k subscribers. Curated daily with listener discovery focus. Include Spotify link and one-line pitch.

name: Ben Nakamura
notes: Long-form analysis podcast. Only covers full albums. Must have cohesive project — not singles. Season lead time is 4+ months.

name: Claire Dupont
notes: Tastemaker blog with influential audience. Strong aesthetic consistency preferred. Streaming-first pitches.

name: Alex Oduya
notes: High-volume music blog. Fast turnaround. One paragraph pitch preferred. Include streaming numbers.

name: Priya Mehta
notes: Academic pop analysis podcast. Prefers artists who can discuss craft and production choices intellectually.

name: Carlos Rivera
notes: Spanish bilingual outlet. Prefers pitches in Spanish. Strong Latin American touring presence helps.

name: Nina Okonkwo
notes: Fanbase trusts deep critical takes. Artwork and production quality scrutinized. Don't pitch if not ready for honest critique.

name: Tyler Mason
notes: Electronic-leaning blog. Club-ready tracks or experimental electronic preferred. Exclusive premieres prioritized.

name: Fatou Diallo
notes: Pan-African music platform. Prefer artists with African roots or strong African influence. French and English pitches accepted.

name: Sam Crawford
notes: Covers critically acclaimed independent artists. Press history and touring required. Not for debut releases.

name: Mia Johansson
notes: 66k subscribers. Curation-focused newsletter. One-paragraph pitch and Spotify link. Fast response rate.

name: Darius Fleming
notes: Deep hip-hop culture podcast. In-person or video interviews preferred. Regional scene knowledge a plus.

name: Eva Stein
notes: Niche but passionate community. Genre authenticity essential — references matter. No mainstream crossover pitches.

name: Jason Wu
notes: Dedicated synth community. Include production credits and gear info if available.

name: Amara Santos
notes: 22k subscribers. Open format for any emerging artist. Include one-line bio and streaming link.

name: Ryan O'Brien
notes: Focuses exclusively on unsigned/independent artists. Include social following and streaming numbers.

name: Layla Hassan
notes: Niche world music blog. Arabic or English pitches. Covers artists from MENA region primarily.

name: Mike Daniels
notes: Punk community outlet. DIY ethos required. No major label artists.

name: Tanya Brooks
notes: 18k subscribers. Faith-based community. Clear spiritual message required. Family-friendly content only.

name: Chris Park
notes: English-language Korean indie podcast. Artists from Korea or Korean diaspora preferred.

name: Diana Flores
notes: Mexican regional music specialist. Spanish-language pitches only. Strong touring in Southwest US and Mexico a plus.

name: Erik Lindqvist
notes: English-language blog covering Nordic music scene. Scandinavian artists or Scandinavian influence preferred.

name: Jasmine Lee
notes: 12k subscribers, jazz community focus. Vinyl and CD releases given extra coverage.

name: Omar Patel
notes: South Asian diaspora community. Bilingual (English/Punjabi) pitches welcomed. Festival performance clips help.

name: Lisa Ngozi
notes: Afrofuturism and genre-blending African music focus. Strong visual aesthetic appreciated.

name: Tom Reyes
notes: Metal community outlet. Genre credibility essential. Include band photo, bio, and tour history.

name: Yara Khalil
notes: 8k subscribers. Arabic-language pitches preferred. Covers Egyptian, Lebanese, Gulf artists primarily.

name: Jake Winters
notes: Traditional bluegrass community. Instrumental prowess highlighted. Festival circuit connections valued.
